items.text: norn-cli crate exists as a workspace member with norn as a dependency
Binary target named 'norn' is declared in Cargo.toml
clap derive used for argument parsing with subcommand support
Cargo.toml declares reedline, clap, dirs, serde_json, tracing-subscriber as dependencies
Default mode is interactive REPL when both stdin and stdout are TTYs
-p / --print flag forces non-interactive print mode
Piped stdin (stdin not a TTY) auto-selects print mode
Piped stdout (stdout not a TTY) auto-selects print mode
Positional PROMPT arguments accepted in both modes
-m / --model flag overrides the profile model
--profile flag loads a Profile from file path or ~/.config/norn/profiles/ by name
-S / --system-prompt flag overrides profile system instructions
--append-system-prompt flag appends to profile system instructions
--allowed-tools flag sets tool allow-list (comma-separated, supports globs)
--disallowed-tools flag sets tool deny-list (comma-separated)
--reasoning-effort flag accepts low/medium/high and threads to LoopContext
--max-turns flag sets AgentLoopConfig::max_iterations
--timeout flag parses duration string and sets AgentLoopConfig::step_timeout
-C / --working-dir flag sets the working directory before agent execution
-c / --config flag accepts KEY=VALUE pairs and maps to AgentLoopConfig/ProviderConfig fields
--rules flag loads a RuleEngine from the specified YAML file
--variables flag sets session variables expanded as {{key}} in system instructions and tool descriptions
-e / --extension flag connects MCP extensions by URI (repeatable)
-s / --output-schema flag accepts inline JSON (starts with {) or file path
--event-schema flag accepts TYPE=JSON|PATH pairs for per-event-type schemas (repeatable)
-f / --output-format flag accepts text, json, or stream-json
text format: final output to stdout, progress/tools to stderr
json format: single JSON envelope to stdout with output, usage, model, session_id, events, result
stream-json format: NDJSON events to stdout (text_delta, tool_call, tool_result, thinking_delta, completed)
-o / --output flag writes final output to file
-q / --quiet flag suppresses progress and tool output on stderr
-r / --resume flag resumes a session by ID or name (no arg = most recent)
--fork flag forks a session by ID or name (no arg = most recent)
--no-session flag disables session persistence for the current run
--session-name flag sets a human-readable name for the session
Sessions persisted as JSONL files at ~/.local/share/norn/sessions/{id}.jsonl
Session index maintained at ~/.local/share/norn/sessions/index.jsonl
Events appended to session file after each run_agent_step call
Session index updated atomically after each append
Resume reconstructs EventStore and message history from JSONL file
Fork copies source events into new session with Fork event appended
XDG_DATA_HOME respected for session storage location
macOS falls back to ~/Library/Application Support/norn/ when XDG vars unset
Piped stdin detected via stdin.is_terminal() — no flag required
Piped stdin content read in full before execution
When both stdin content and positional PROMPT exist, stdin is prepended as context with <stdin> delimiters
reedline line editor used for interactive input
Multi-line input supported via configurable key (Shift+Enter or Alt+Enter)
Command history persisted to ~/.local/share/norn/history.txt
Tab completion for slash commands
Tab completion for file paths
Prompt displays model name and session status
Ctrl+C interrupts current agent step
Ctrl+D exits the REPL
Multi-turn conversation maintains EventStore across turns
LoopContext dynamic sections cleared between turns, prompt commands re-evaluated
Slash commands processed via preprocess_input() in both REPL and print mode
/help lists available commands with descriptions
/tools lists available tools with names and descriptions
/model with no arg shows current model; with arg switches model
/schema with no arg shows current output schema; with arg sets it
/compact triggers real context compaction via ContextEdits::auto_compact_keeping_recent_turns
/clear resets the EventStore and message history
/session shows session ID, name, turn count, and cumulative token usage
/name sets a human-readable name for the current session
/variables lists active session variables and their values
/exit and /quit exit the REPL
Profile-registered slash commands loaded from SlashCommandRegistry
CLI subscribes to broadcast::Sender<ProviderEvent> for real-time streaming
Text deltas streamed to stdout character-by-character in REPL mode
Thinking deltas rendered dimmed on stderr
Tool call summary displayed when tool call is complete (tool name + key arguments)
Tool results displayed with head/tail truncation for long output
Schema validation feedback shown on stderr when schema attempt fails
Token usage summary (input/output tokens, elapsed time) shown after each step
Profile resolved from file path (contains / or .) or name from ~/.config/norn/profiles/
from_profile() used to build LoopContext and gated ToolRegistry
CLI flags override profile values (model, system prompt, tools, reasoning effort)
Per-event schemas from profile merged with --event-schema CLI flags
Profile prompt_commands threaded into LoopContext
Profile capabilities resolved into tool allow-list, instructions, and disallowed patterns
norn auth login triggers OAuth PKCE flow via norn::provider::auth::login()
norn auth logout clears credentials via norn::provider::auth::logout()
norn auth status reports login state, token expiry, and account ID without exposing tokens
--codex-home flag on auth login overrides the codex home directory
norn session list shows sessions from current directory (--all for all)
norn session list supports --limit and --format (table or json)
norn session show displays session metadata and event summary
norn session resume enters REPL with resumed session
norn session fork creates new session from existing and enters REPL
norn session export serializes session to jsonl, json, or markdown
norn session remove deletes session file and index entry
Session ID accepts partial prefix (minimum 8 characters) for disambiguation
norn mcp serve starts Norn as MCP server on stdio with full tool registry
norn mcp connect tests connection to external MCP server and reports capabilities
norn doctor checks OAuth status
norn doctor checks provider connectivity
norn doctor checks working directory permissions
norn doctor reports pass/fail with actionable remediation messages
norn completion generates scripts for bash, zsh, and fish via clap_complete
Exit code 0 on successful completion
Exit code 1 on agent error (provider failure, tool error, schema unreachable)
Exit code 2 on CLI argument error
Exit code 3 on authentication error
OpenAiProvider constructed with OAuth auth source by default
Base URL auto-detected from AuthSource (ChatGPT backend for OAuth, api.openai.com for API key)
Provider config respects -c overrides for base_url, max_retries, request_timeout, and provider_options
--provider flag selects backend: openai (default) or claude-runner (ClaudeRunnerAdapter)
SimpleTokenEstimator wired into LoopContext::token_estimator unconditionally
ContextEdits::new() wired into LoopContext::context_edits unconditionally
RetryPolicy constructed from -c retry_max and retry_base_delay overrides (default: no retry)
DiagnosticCollector constructed and drained at step completion
Diagnostics rendered on stderr in text mode, included in json envelope, emitted as events in stream-json
IterationMonitorConfig loaded from profile iteration_monitor section
-c retry_max maps to RetryPolicy::max_retries (loop-level), distinct from -c max_retries (HTTP-level ProviderConfig)
-c request_timeout maps to ProviderConfig::timeout (per-request HTTP timeout)
-c provider_options accepts inline JSON mapped to ProviderConfig::provider_options
CLI constructs DiagnosticCollector and sets it on LoopContext::diagnostics before run_agent_step
DiagnosticCollector published on ToolContext via ToolRegistry::set_context with insert_extension
Profile tool_config section parsed for per-tool validation thresholds (max_code_lines, glob overrides)
-c write.max_code_lines=N sets WriteTool length limit from CLI override
Profile tool_config for Write constructs LengthLimit with specified default and glob overrides
DiagnosticCollector drained after run_agent_step and rendered per output format (text=stderr, json=envelope, stream-json=events)
